title: Space Tech Briefing — May 21, 2026
summary:
Good morning, future voyagers and space enthusiasts! It's May 21, 2026, and we are kicking off your day with the latest, most exciting developments from across our solar system. Get ready for a blast of space news!

First up, we have a fantastic update from NASA's groundbreaking Psyche mission! The Psyche probe, launched back in October 2023, just aced a critical milestone on its epic journey to the metallic asteroid 16-Psyche. This intrepid explorer performed a precision gravity assist maneuver around Mars on May 15th! This wasn't just a flyby; it was a crucial slingshot, designed to boost its speed and fine-tune its trajectory. Psyche is now hurtling even faster towards its target, an asteroid believed to be the exposed core of an early planetesimal. It's a journey spanning nearly 2.4 billion kilometers, with orbital insertion around 16-Psyche anticipated in July 2029. What a feat of celestial mechanics!

And speaking of Mars, let's look back at a major recent development we've been tracking, one that's building up to an incredibly exciting launch later this year. The Japan Aerospace Exploration Agency, JAXA, is making final preparations for its Martian Moons eXploration, or MMX, mission! The MMX probe has arrived at its launch site in Japan. This is the ultimate stage before its highly anticipated departure to Mars and its mysterious moons, Phobos and Deimos, which is slated for October 2026. Get ready for a close-up look at these enigmatic bodies!

What a time to be alive, witnessing humanity's relentless drive to explore the cosmos! That's all for your morning space briefing. Stay curious, keep looking up, and join us again soon for more cosmic updates. Have a stellar day!
